title: Crab-harp

'''Crab-harps''' were [[Bioengineering/Legends|bioengineered]] [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] creatures created by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. [[Crab/Legends|Crab]]-[[Harp/Legends|harps]] had long [[Limb/Legends|legs]] with tendons that linked the tips of each of their legs to their bodies. When their legs were locked in an extended position, the stretched tendons could be used to create [[Music/Legends|music]] by plucking them. In existence by [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]], during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], these [[Crustacean/Legends|crustaceans]] were used by the Yuuzhan Vong as musical instruments or were set loose at ceremonies to make their own music.

==Biology and appearance==
[[Bioengineering/Legends|Bioengineered]] by the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]],<ref name="Crab">{{CSWECite|I|150|crab-harp}}</ref> [[Crab/Legends|crab]]-[[Harp/Legends|harps]] were long-[[Limb/Legends|limbed]] [[Crustacean/Legends|crustaceans]]<ref name="Balance" /> that were about the size of a Yuuzhan Vong torso,<ref name="Guide" /> and they had translucent tendons that connected the tips of each limb to the body. [[Color/Legends|Colored]] red on their limbs<ref name="Balance" /> and gray on their bodies,<ref name="Guide" /> crab-harps could lock their limbs in an extended position so that the tendons were stretched tight; in this position, the crustacean resembled a type of harp. Stroking or plucking the tendons produced an eerie, atonal [[Music/Legends|music]].<ref name="Balance" />

==Behavior==
Classified as non-sentient, crab-harps allowed Yuuzhan Vong to play them as musical instruments when their legs were locked in their extended positions.<ref name="Balance" /> When set to roam free, they played themselves by rubbing their legs together to create music.<ref name="Crab" />

==History==
[[File:Priests_and_crab-harp.png|thumb|right|200px|Several priests, one of whom holds a crab-harp]]
The Yuuzhan Vong had [[Gene/Legends|genetically]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineered]] crab-harps for the sound of their music<ref name="Crab" /> by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]].<ref name="Guide" /> At that [[Time/Legends|time]], the Yuuzhan Vong were in the process of [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invading]] the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]–controlled [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="Balance" /> Crab-harps were widely used and participated in ceremonies undertaken by Yuuzhan Vong [[Priest/Legends|priests]],<ref name="Music">{{FactFile|131|MUS 1-4|Music and Musicians}}</ref> with the crustacean musical instruments either being played by attendants<ref name="Balance" /> or set loose to roam freely in order to create background music.<ref name="Crab" />

In 26 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]],<ref name="Guide" /> a trio of attendants used two crab-harps during the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Duro (Yuuzhan Vong War)|attack]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Duro/Legends|Duro]]. There, the creatures made music as Yuuzhan Vong [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] the captured [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], a representative of the New Republic.<ref name="Balance" /> Later in the war, crab-harps were used to provide music as part of a [[Grand Ceremony|grand ceremony]] that took place in the [[Place of Sacrifice]] on the [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|captured]] world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Crab-harps first appeared in the novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Balance Point]]'', released in [[2000]] and written by [[Kathy Tyers]]. ''Balance Point'' was a part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series of books.<ref name="Balance" /> Crab-harps were also mentioned in the [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 131|131st]] installment of ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File]]''. However, the ''Fact File'' incorrectly describes that two crab-harps were connected with their tendons to another [[Yuuzhan Vong drum critter|drum]] creature to create a large musical instrument.<ref name="Fact File">{{FactFile|131|MUS 1-4|Music and Musicians}}</ref> As all of the other sources describe the crab-harp as not being attached to the drum creature, this article assumes that the ''Fact File'' is in error.
